'''Maks Leem''' was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Gran/Legends|Gran]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] who served the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] during the waning [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].

==Biography==
Maks Leem, a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Gran/Legends|Gran]] from the [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Malastare/Legends|Malastare]], was trained in the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], achieving the rank of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] before the start of the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]], a political schism in the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]]. Appointed as an [[Jedi instructor|instructor]] at the [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]], Master Leem [[Jedi training/Legends|trained]] two [[Padawan/Legends|Padawans]] to the rank of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] before the civil unrest engulfed [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], Jedi [[Rees Alrix]] and [[Eremin Tarn]]. An expert in [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] navigation, Master Leem instructed classes at the Temple, as well as working in the Temple's [[Jedi Temple refectory/Legends|refectory]]. The Gran Master, while not a fan of [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]], had crafted for herself a blue-bladed [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]].<ref name="YDR" />

When the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] erupted between the Republic and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], Master Leem was one of nearly [[Jedi assault team/Legends|two hundred Jedi]] who fought in the opening [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|battle]] on [[Geonosis/Legends|Geonosis]], a conflict which introduced the Jedi to the newly formed [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]]. Returning to a nearly empty Temple, Master Leem remained on Coruscant for much of the war as an instructor while most of her peers were scattered around [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] fighting. Early in the war, Master Leem's last Padawan, Jedi Eremin Tarn, willingly left the Order, joining Jedi [[Sian Jeisel]]'s schismatic movement of Jedi refusing to fight. At this time, Master Leem began the training of her third Padawan, the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Whie Malreaux/Legends|Whie Malreaux]], one of the Temple's most promising students. The pair remained on Coruscant as Leem continued to teach and Malreaux served at the [[Jedi Temple/Legends#Layout and architecture|Temple's main entrance]] as part of the [[Temple Security Force]].<ref name="YDR" />

In the days leading up to the [[Jedi Temple Apprentice Tournament|annual Apprentice Tournament]], a [[Troxan]] [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomat]] delivered an [[incendiary packet]] for [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] into the hands of Master Leem, who in turn rushed the packet to the [[Room of a Thousand Fountains/Legends|Room of a Thousand Fountains]]. There, Master Yoda safely opened the package to reveal the damaged [[Lightsaber hilt/Legends|lightsaber hilt]] of Jedi [[Jang Li-Li]]. Saddened by Li-Li's inevitable death, Yoda and Leem talked for a time, with the old Master jokingly assigning Leem the task of locating a new home for the Temple. In the following weeks, Master Leem and her Padawan were assigned to a [[Mission to Vjun (Clone Wars)|mission]], accompanied by Masters Yoda, [[Jai Maruk]] and his Padawan, [[Tallisibeth Enwandung-Esterhazy]]. Leaving by way of the [[Chancellor Palpatine Spaceport]], the Jedi smuggled Yoda offworld in a hollowed out [[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|R2-series astromech droid]] aboard the ''[[Reasonable Doubt]]'' and headed for [[Vjun/Legends|Vjun]], where Yoda was to meet with [[Count of Serenno/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]], leader of the Confederacy.<ref name="YDR" />

When the ''Reasonable Doubt'' stopped to refuel at [[Phindar Station]], the Jedi were [[Battle of the Phindar Spaceport|ambushed]] by several [[A-series assassin droid|assassin droids]] armed with an eclectic array of weaponry. Activating two [[hard-sound gun]]s, the droids crippled Masters Maruk and Leem; both dropped to their knees as the hard-sound ruptured their eardrums. Master Leem, having dropped her lightsaber and covering her ears as she screamed in pain, fought through her agony and retrieved her lightsaber. Cutting loose a chunk of [[Durasteel/Legends|durasteel]], she hurled it with the Force and destroyed one of the auditory-assault weapons. Allowing years of practice to take over, the two Masters rushed the droids, two against eighteen.<ref name="YDR" />

Master Leem, trained in the art of lightsaber combat as were all Jedi, realized that her aversion to war had led her to avoid combat training, and that her skills as a fighter were diminished. Quickly receiving a leg wound due to one of the droids' [[Flechette launcher/Legends|flechette launchers]], she lost her lightsaber once more when her sword arm was badly damaged by more shards of metal. Destroying two droids with her mastery of the Force, Master Leem was in the midst of looking for Master Yoda and the Padawans when [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Asajj Ventress/Legends|Asajj Ventress]] arrived on the concourse and began to mock the Jedi. Engaging Master Maruk as Leem struggled to remain conscious, Ventress was faster than the Human Jedi and quickly subdued him. As Maruk struggled to remain in the light of the Force, Ventress taunted him by pouncing on Master Leem, slashing the dying Gran in the chest. Disregarding the wound, Master Leem focused on her struggling companion, begging him to stay on the Jedi Path. As Leem struggled to maintain her position on the ground as a large hole in the station began to slowly vent oxygen and drag her from the room, Master Maruk, determined to stay in the light, denied the dark side. Master Leem, unable to hear or look at the man as she focused on sealing the breach in the hull, felt his resolve in the Force and was content, knowing that both would die as Jedi. Patching the breach as Maruk was cut in half by Ventress, Leem lay helpless on the ground as the [[Dathomirian/Legends|Dathomirian]] approached the Gran and stabbed her in the chest, killing the Jedi Master.<ref name="YDR" />

==Personality and traits==
A Gran, Master Maks Leem displayed the community-oriented mind of her people. The socially minded woman enjoyed her life as a Jedi. Described by her students and peers as motherly and calm, Master Leem was not happy when the Jedi were forced into the militant role they served in the Clone Wars.<ref name="YDR" />

Master Leem spent much of her day in deep [[Meditation/Legends|meditation]], burning a richly scented incense to help induce a deep trance. Additionally, it was necessary for Master Leem to be alone at this time due to the din caused by the digestive tract that processed the contents of the Gran's four stomachs.<ref name="YDR" />

Master Leem had a habit of grinding her teeth when she was anxious, a trait carried over from the Gran's cud-chewing past.<ref name="YDR" />

==Behind the scenes==
Maks Leem first appeared in ''[[Yoda: Dark Rendezvous]]'', a novel written by [[Sean Stewart]] and published in [[2004]].<ref name="YDR" /> She was among the limited characters available in the mobile game ''[[Star Wars: Battle for the Republic]]''. Leem later received an entry in the [[2008]] ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.
